key_insight: HCC-STAR is a clinically aligned LLM that turns routine HCC EMR narratives into risk staging, guideline-consistent treatment rankings, and individualized survival estimates.
clinical_relevance: In a multi-center evaluation, it aimed to improve precision treatment guidance beyond coarse staging systems and support faster, more accurate physician decision-making.
method: Trained on ~30,000 SEER-derived HCC cases expanded into EMR-style narratives with clinician-validated prompt-based augmentation.
Used a knowledge-aligned reasoning framework with step-verifiable composite rewards.
Output risk-score staging, ranked treatment options with rationales, and survival estimates.
evidence_signal: In 6,668 patients across 12 Chinese hospitals, HCC-STAR reportedly outperformed guideline baselines and competitive models on treatment recommendation and risk stratification, with clinician ratings favoring its reasoning quality.
watch_item: Promising results come from retrospective, multi-center evaluation, so real-world safety, generalizability, and workflow integration still need prospective validation.
paper_title: Towards Precision Therapy in Hepatocellular Carcinoma: A Clinical-Reasoning LLM for Risk Stratification and Treatment Guidance
